ehcp.name: Education, Health and Care Plan (EHCP)
ehcp.description: A legal document that describes a child's special educational, health and social care needs and the support they will get.

home_to_school_transport.name: Home-to-School Transport
home_to_school_transport.eligibility: Children with SEN or disabilities who cannot reasonably be expected to walk to school.
home_to_school_transport.support: Free transport (bus, taxi, or mileage allowance) provided by the Local Authority.

disabled_students_allowance_dsa.name: Disabled Students' Allowance (DSA)
disabled_students_allowance_dsa.description: Support for higher education students with a learning difficulty, health condition or disability.

pupil_premium.name: Pupil Premium
pupil_premium.description: Funding for schools to help disadvantaged pupils of all abilities to perform better, and close the gap between them and their peers.
pupil_premium.eligibility: Pupils currently eligible for Free School Meals, or who have been at any point in the last 6 years ('Ever 6'), LAC, and PLAC.

free_school_meals.name: Free School Meals (FSM)
free_school_meals.infant_fsm: All children in Reception, Year 1, and Year 2 in government-funded schools are entitled to free school meals.

eligibility_criteria.description: For older children, eligibility depends on the household receiving certain benefits.
other_qualifying_benefits: Support under Part VI of the Immigration and Asylum Act 1999
The guaranteed element of Pension Credit
Child Tax Credit (if not receiving Working Tax Credit and income < £16,190)

vulnerable_student_bursary.eligibility: Includes those receiving DLA or PIP in their own right and Universal Credit or ESA.

school_uniform_grant.name: School Uniform Grant
school_uniform_grant.description: Financial help to buy school uniform for children from low-income families.
school_uniform_grant.note: Discretionary and provided by some local councils in England.

early_years_pupil_premium.name: Early Years Pupil Premium (EYPP)
early_years_pupil_premium.eligibility: 3 and 4-year-olds receiving the free entitlement and meeting the same criteria as Pupil Premium (e.g. FSM).

disability_access_fund.name: Disability Access Fund (DAF)
disability_access_fund.eligibility: 3 and 4-year-olds who are receiving the free entitlement and are in receipt of Disability Living Allowance (DLA).

notional_sen_budget.description: The amount of money a school receives to support its pupils with SEN. Schools are expected to fund the first £6,000 of additional support for a child with SEN from this budget.

top_up_funding.name: Top-up Funding (Element 3)
top_up_funding.description: Funding provided by the local authority for children with high-level needs (usually those with an EHCP) that cost more than the school's notional SEN budget covers.

holiday_activity_and_food_programme.name: Holiday Activity and Food (HAF) Programme
holiday_activity_and_food_programme.description: Provides healthy food and enriching activities to children who receive benefits-related free school meals during the school holidays.
holiday_activity_and_food_programme.eligibility: Children receiving benefits-related Free School Meals.

short_breaks.name: Short Breaks (Respite)
short_breaks.description: Support to give parents a break from caring and children a chance to take part in activities.
short_breaks.eligibility: Assessments by children's social care services.

max_card.name: Max Card
max_card.description: Discount card for foster carers and families of children with additional needs to visit attractions at a lower cost.
